source.title: Probabilistic classification learning with corrective feedback is selectively impaired in early Huntington's disease — evidence for the role of the striatum in learning with feedback.

confidence_rationale: Direct, well-controlled clinical test of the HD half of MECH-172's double dissociation. The feedback-vs-paired-associate manipulation is the right test for whether striatum is specifically required for the model-free RL component (it is). Mapping fidelity is high because the paper isolates the HD/striatal-loss limb that MECH-172 invokes. Source quality is solid (Neuropsychologia, n=18 early-HD with matched controls). Transfer risk is moderate -- early HD already includes 50% caudate MSN loss, so the striatal-causation inference is reasonable.
failure_signatures: If MECH-172's architectural separation were wrong, HD patients should be impaired on the paired-associate (declarative) version too. They were not.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Early Huntington patients are selectively impaired on feedback-based probabilistic classification but not on the paired-associate version of the same task. Striatum is specifically required for learning with corrective feedback.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-172's HD half: pure habit-system disorders (HD, striatal lesions) impair model-free RL while leaving the hippocampal/declarative pipeline relatively intact. Holl's feedback-vs-PA manipulation is the cleanest available isolation of that selective impairment.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Holl's selective impairment is on feedback-based RL, not on motor-sequence procedural memory in general. MECH-172 talks more broadly about habit and procedural memory; the read-across assumes that feedback-based RL is the relevant model-free component. There is also a confound the authors discuss: HD patients may have additional executive/working-memory load that interacts with feedback monitoring.
mapping.source_context: 18 early HD patients vs 18 matched controls; weather-prediction task in two versions (feedback / paired-associate).

confidence_components.notes: Sample size moderate; design well-controlled. Early-HD avoids the late-stage cortical involvement that would muddy the BG-causation reading.
